text:
{
  "title": "External Health Archetype Affinity",
  "group_name": "external_health_archetype_affinity",
  "family": "external_prototype_similarity",
  "summary": "Represent each row by how closely its complete lifestyle and physiology profile resembles labeled health-condition archetypes learned only from the auxiliary student-health source.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Use the auxiliary student_health_dataset_50k.csv.gz as the only labeled reference source, aligning the shared project feature columns and ignoring student_id and timestamp; this follows the retrieved student lifestyle clustering motivation from https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/16/24/4339 and the source dataset context at https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/ziya07/college-student-health-behavior-dataset. For each target class in the auxiliary data, compute numeric feature medians; compute one global numeric scale per feature as max(IQR, 1e-6), winsorizing row values to the auxiliary 1st and 99th percentiles before scoring. For every project row and class, create a numeric archetype distance as the mean over observed numeric features of min(6, abs(value - class_median) / global_IQR). For categorical features, compute smoothed per-class category log-lift using (class_count_for_value + 1) / (class_total + number_of_levels) divided by the corresponding smoothed overall category rate; for a row and class, average these log-lifts over observed categorical fields. Missing numeric or categorical values are excluded from that component denominator; if no fields are observed for a component, set that component to 0. Emit classwise combined affinity scores defined as categorical_log_lift minus numeric_distance for unhealthy, at-risk, and fit, plus the best-minus-second-best affinity margin and the fit-minus-unhealthy affinity contrast.",
  "expected_signal": "The external labels can encode source-style class archetypes for minority classes without using project target labels, so these balanced class-affinity features may help separate fit and unhealthy cases that raw one-feature thresholds or cumulative burden summaries blur into the dominant at-risk pattern.",
  "risk": "The auxiliary file is missing-free and heavily at-risk, so prototype affinities may be source-shifted or too coarse for project rows with substantial missingness; the implementation must not use project health_condition labels when building the reference statistics."
}
